prevalence.value: 1.2% (annual point prevalence, including community-acquired pneumonia [CAP] at 1.0% and hospital-acquired pneumonia [HAP] at 0.2%)
prevalence.source: DataSUS/SIM and Ministério da Saúde estimates, extrapolated from 2022 hospitalization data
prevalence.trend: Stable with slight decline in CAP due to pneumococcal vaccination (PCV13 coverage >90% in children)

incidence.value: 12.5 per 1,000 population (CAP: 10.5 per 1,000; HAP: 2.0 per 1,000 hospitalized patients)
incidence.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 (IHME) adjusted for Brazilian DATASUS 2023 data

burden.dalyPerCapita: 0.015 DALYs per capita (lower respiratory infections including pneumonia)
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2021 update, with DATASUS mortality registry

riskFactors: Age extremes (children <5 years and adults >65 years)
Nosocomial factors like mechanical ventilation (for HAP)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (fever, cough, dyspnea) + radiological evidence of pulmonary infiltrate per Brazilian Thoracic Society guidelines; microbiological confirmation for severe cases (CAP vs HAP distinction based on onset >48 hours post-admission for HAP)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine screening; symptomatic diagnosis via SUS (Sistema Único de Saúde) primary care (coverage ~70% in urban areas)
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: 2.7 million new cases annually (based on incidence rate)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Antibiotic stewardship ~60%; pneumococcal vaccine coverage 85% in target groups (children and elderly)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted WHO guidelines with emphasis on rapid antigen tests for Streptococcus pneumoniae in SUS facilities; HAP surveillance mandatory in ICUs per ANVISA
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Empirical antibiotics (amoxicillin-clavulanate for CAP; piperacillin-tazobactam for HAP) per Ministério da Saúde protocols; oxygen and supportive care widely available in SUS hospitals
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Higher incidence in North/Northeast (up to 15 per 1,000) due to socioeconomic disparities, limited access in rural areas, and antimicrobial resistance (e.g., 30% MRSA in HAP); seasonal peaks during rainy season
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal SUS coverage ensures free treatment; national vaccination program reduced pediatric CAP by 40% since 2010; robust DATASUS surveillance for outbreaks
